notes: Two back-to-back no-op turns ('Reply with exactly: ok') in ONE live aider REPL session, locking down the multi-turn TOKEN ACCUMULATOR. After every model call aider prints a `> Tokens: N sent, M received.` blockquote into .aider.chat.history.md; the aider parser's tokensRE matches it and closeModelCall emits one assistant_message carrying a PerTurnContribution{Usage:{Input:sent,Output:received}} + TokenSnapshot{Total:sent+received}, which the tailer sums into a MONOTONIC SessionMetrics.TotalTokens (core/pkg/tailer/tailer_metrics.go). OBSERVABLE part of the scenario (what this cell proves): TotalTokens > 0 after turn 1, and TotalTokens after turn 2 ≥ TotalTokens after turn 1 (non-decreasing). Two turns give exactly one accumulation boundary (T1→T2) to witness the non-decreasing invariant. UNOBSERVABLE part (observes:partial scope_note, deliberately NOT asserted here): the cache-bucket distinction (CacheReadTokens > 0; fresh input = 0 for a fully-cached turn). Aider's cache report (`Tokens: … cache write … cache hit …`) is emitted via io.tool_output to the TERMINAL ONLY — show_usage_report never append_chat_history's it — so the bare `N sent, M received.` form is all that reaches the markdown that irrlicht watches (FilesUnderCWD). And even if a cache segment did land, tokensRE captures only sent/received (cache text falls into the unused rest group) so UsageBreakdown.CacheRead/CacheCreation stay 0. The cache-distinction signal therefore cannot fire for aider — that is the observes:partial carve-out, covered by the assessment, not by a phase here. WHY the phase DSL asserts only the lifecycle arc: the token-accumulation claim (TotalTokens > 0 / non-decreasing) has no metric form in the invariant DSL today; it is covered out-of-band by the aider parser unit tests (parser_test.go: TestParser_MultipleTokensLines, TestParser_MultiTurn_EmitsAssistantMessagePerTurn). expected.jsonl asserts the two-turn lifecycle arc the accounting rides on — two ready→working→ready cycles on the SAME session — so a recording that drifts from that arc (e.g. turns merge, no per-turn reset) fails here even though the metric itself isn't phase-checkable. WHY interactive (not headless `aider --message`): headless aider is single-shot and exits after one reply, so it can't show the cross-turn accumulation boundary; the interactive driver keeps the REPL alive across both turns via send+wait_turn and kills the tmux session once at the end → a SINGLE process_exited. AIDER TURN-END IS SYNTHESIZED, NOT MARKED: a `> Tokens:` line closes one model CALL, not the turn; end-of-turn is synthesized OUT-OF-BAND by IdleFlush after 1500ms quiet, surfaced behind the daemon's 5s SessionDetector poll (~5s effective working→ready latency), so the inter-turn / trailing sleeps are ≥7s — short sleeps let the next #### prompt re-enter working before IdleFlush fires and the per-turn reset is lost (TestParser_NewUserPromptAfterMultiCall_StaysWorking). LAZY TRANSCRIPT: .aider.chat.history.md materializes only once the first user input lands, so the live REPL is observable from launch as a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready (the process scanner) before the per-cwd transcript appears — session_birth (which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s') legitimately matches that pre-session. The TURN ARCS must land on the session that goes working: t1_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording (the pre-session never goes working); every later turn phase, pid_bind, and teardown pin to t1_start's session via same_session_as so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, and sub-second timing vary (model-output variance); the per-turn `sent`/`received` numbers are model-dependent but the SUM is non-decreasing by construction. Stable structurally: two ready→working→ready arcs on one session, a single process_exited, a final ready state. NOTE: this spec is the DOCUMENTED TARGET — to be validated when aider auth is available in-env (no provider key present at authoring time; headless aider hangs ~5min on OpenRouter OAuth and the rig precheck delegates auth to the user).

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch (the proc-<PID> pre-session; the lazy transcript has not materialized yet)
First prompt → working (binds the live aider session; the pre-session never enters working). Turn 1's `> Tokens:` line lands the first PerTurnContribution → TotalTokens > 0 after this turn
First turn ends → back to ready (IdleFlush synthesizes turn-end after the prompt goes quiet); state reset between turns, never entered waiting
Second user prompt → working again on the same session
Second turn ends → back to ready; turn 2's `> Tokens:` line accumulates → TotalTokens after T2 ≥ TotalTokens after T1 (monotonic across the one accumulation boundary)
PID bound to the live aider session (aider binds its PID at birth, before the first turn — anchored to recording start, not t1_start)
REPL torn down once after the second turn → a single process_exited on the session; state immediately before teardown is ready
